title: Cardamine koreana
intro: Cardamine koreana, also known as Korean bittercress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Korean medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cardamine koreana include isothiocyanates, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been found to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. In herbal medicine, Cardamine koreana is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to make a medicinal tonic.
taxonomy: Cardamine koreana is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Brassicaceae. It belongs to the genus Cardamine, which consists of various species of spring beauty or bittercress plants. The species Cardamine koreana is native to Korea and is often found growing in moist, shaded areas. This plant is also known by the synonym Cardamine amplexidens. The classification of Cardamine koreana is further supported by its morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic analysis.
